label: Squarish 3BR / baths CLUSTERED, FRONT_BACK split with mid-band HALL — full-width public band at front (living-kitchen, lk style, dining folded into living), full-width private band at rear with all three bedrooms; wet core (common + ensuite back-to-back) in the middle band beside the hall. The most compact of the 3BR squarish family.

adjacencies.note: lk style — living and kitchen are one open front band, dining folds into the living side
hall's south mouth opens into the living room — no door
ensuite sits in the mid band directly in front of master — interior door on master's front wall
back-to-back baths share one plumbing wall
common bath backs onto the kitchen wall — all three wet rooms in one contiguous stack
master door at the hall's east end
br2 door opens north into the hall
br3 door opens north into the hall
common bath door opens west into the hall

soft_proximities.note: keep the shared bath centered on the standard pair

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_front_back_split_baths_cl_hall_lk
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: This is the economic workhorse of the 3BR set, so the trigger stays at 100 m². The rear band carries three bedroom widths (>= 2.8 + 2.8 + 3.0 m), so master is capped hardest (14 m²). Baths sit in preferred so they can compress toward the hard min on BP 220-class shells while bedroom widths hold.

notes: 3BR squarish catalog topology #3 — FRONT_BACK split, clustered wet stack.
The most compact of the four: natural floor band ~84-96 m²; minimum feasible
shell ~9.3 x 9.0 m (width is the binding constraint, not area). Verified
2026-07-18 end-to-end (solve + validate COMPLIANT) for ncp, ccp right,
ccp left (mirror) and fcp, down to a true 9.0 x 9.0 m buildable (ncp).
Layout intent (canonical, brief.carport_side='right'):
Rear:  [br2   | br3          | master          ]
Mid:   [HALL          | common | ensuite       ]
Front: [living (entry)         | kitchen       ]
Program: clean two-band parti. Full-width public band at front — living
hosts entry, kitchen at front-right, lk open plan (dining folds into living;
the lanai or dirty-kitchen side can host overflow dining). Full-width private
band at rear holds all three bedrooms. The thin middle band carries the
east-west hall plus the entire wet stack: kitchen, common and ensuite form
one contiguous plumbing bay (kitchen fronts it, common backs the kitchen,
ensuite backs common and doors into master).
Ventilation: all three bedrooms hold the rear wall (windows to the rear
setback / service area), living + kitchen hold the front — no landlocked
room. Baths and hall vent via high windows or mechanical, per PD 1096
allowance for baths.
Fit rule: rear band needs >= 8.6 m internal width (2.8 + 2.8 + 3.0) —
shell width >= ~9.3 m with wall allowances. Depth can compress to ~9.0 m
because the mid band is only bath-depth (~1.8-2.0 m).
Single wet stack = cheapest plumbing of the family; pick this topology first
for BP 220 / economic-housing briefs. When brief.carport_side='left', mirror.
Solver notes (2026-07-18): left_anchored/right_anchored were REMOVED —
as authored (living+br2 left, kitchen+ensuite+master right) the topology
was infeasible at every lot size (confirmed up to 20x20 buildable). Root
cause: any anchor-list combination beyond a single list alone contradicts
the front_to_rear_stacks + full adjacency graph (hall alone touches 5
other rooms while chained front-to-rear between living and br2) — pinning
kitchen/ensuite/master flush to the right wall was the specific trigger,
but left+rear together (no right at all) also failed. rear_anchored alone
is sufficient: kitchen_side='right' (the solver's softer center-based
symmetry break, untouched) still reliably keeps living left / kitchen
right in practice, but br2/br3 left-right order within the rear band is
no longer pinned — solver's choice, sometimes br3|br2|master instead of
the diagram's br2|br3|master. Functionally equivalent (both are plain
standard bedrooms); treat the ASCII diagram above as intent, not a
geometry guarantee.
